reasoning: Trump publicly expressed a desire in September 2025 to reclaim Bagram Airbase, but this was firmly rejected by the Taliban and regional neighbors (like Russia and India). Defense experts note that re-occupying Bagram would constitute a major re-invasion requiring over 10,000 troops and substantial logistical buildup since Afghanistan is landlocked. As of late November 2025, no such buildup is occurring. Furthermore, in early October, a Pentagon official explicitly denied rumors of a return, stating there are 'no plans or negotiations to re-establish a military footprint' in Afghanistan. With only a little over a month remaining in the year, the logistical impossibility of staging and executing a major military invasion from a cold start makes this event extremely unlikely.
